master_row.title: Wavefunction Evolution Equation
master_row.canonical_statement: \begin{theorem}[Spiral Pitch Angle from Accumulated Curvature] The pitch angle $\psi(r)$ of galactic spiral arms relates to accumulated spacetime curvature through: \begin{equation} \tan[\psi(r)] = \frac{r}{v_{\text{pattern}}} \left|\frac{\partial\Omega}{\partial r}\right| \sqrt{1 - \frac{r_s}{r}} \left(1 + \frac{a^2}{r^2}\right)^{1/4} \times \mathcal{A}(r,t) \end{equation} where the accumulation factor: \begin{equation} \mathcal{A}(r,t) = 1 + \int_0^t \frac{8\pi G}{c^4} T_{\text{spiral}}(r,\tau) e^{-(t-\tau)/\tau_{\text{gal}}} d\tau \end{equation} with $\tau_{\text{gal}} \sim 10^8$ years representing the galactic decoherence timescale. \end{theorem}
master_row.formation_note: Recovered from a D:\All source document under ‘Curvature-Spiral Correspondence’.
master_row.dictionary: Wavefunction Evolution Equation — tan[ψ(r)] = fracrv_pattern |(∂Ω)/(∂ r) | √(1 - (r_s)/(r)) (1 + (a^2)/(r^2) )^1/4 × A(r,t)
master_row.encyclopedia: The entry preserves the equation exactly as extracted from `D:\00_Engine\CSV\emergent_curvature_theory.tex` for registry alignment and later derivation.
